hawkish_phrases: CPI inflation is expected to pick up further above the target...and is likely to exceed 3% for a temporary period
Global price pressures have picked up further, reflecting strong demand for goods, rising commodity prices, supply-side constraints and transportation bottlenecks
vacancies have risen above pre-Covid levels, and there are increasing signs of recruitment difficulties
private sector regular pay in the three months to April was 5.6% higher than a year earlier
Twelve-month CPI inflation rose from 1.5% in April to 2.1% in May, above the MPC's 2% target
dovish_phrases: The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy at least until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
There is judged to be spare capacity in aggregate in the economy at present
The Committee's expectation is that the direct impact of rises in commodity prices on CPI inflation will be transitory
the Committee judges that UK inflation expectations remain well anchored
will take whatever action is necessary to achieve its remit
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.1% with an 8-1 vote on asset purchases, maintaining dovish forward guidance that tightening will not occur until spare capacity is eliminated, despite acknowledging inflation will temporarily exceed 3%.
